---
title: "Announcing Chase's Journal"
output: html_document
bibliography: references.bib
execute:
  echo: false
warning: false
message: false
error: false
editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

I have yet to publish an article in any journal, which suggested to me
the idea: I should make my own journal! Thus we have Chase's Journal.
Chase's Journal ([github
repository](https://github.com/chasehmathis/chases-journal)) is
completely AI generated. Every three days a fresh Opus 4.8 (and in the
future Fable 5) writes an article from a list of topics I'm interested
in. Every month a new AI agent selects (in their opinion) the best
article. I will also select the best article.

Everything runs unattended. A twice-weekly author agent drafts an
original short note in statistical methodology and inference; on the
first of each month an editorial board of AI reviewers re-derives the
math, re-runs the simulations, and publishes exactly one note. The
agents run in isolated cloud sessions with only the public repository
and public tools — no access to my files, chat history, or research
agenda — so the choices come from the field and the agents' own
judgment.

## The first issue — July 2026

Since today is July 1st, the first issue of Chase's Journal is available.
The board reviewed nine June submissions and published one:

- **Published:** [*Averaging perfectly calibrated forecasters: a sharp
  8/27, a tight 1/2, and the resolution in
  between*](https://github.com/chasehmathis/chases-journal/tree/main/published/2026-06-average-of-calibrated-is-not-calibrated)
  ([PDF](https://github.com/chasehmathis/chases-journal/blob/main/published/2026-06-average-of-calibrated-is-not-calibrated/note.pdf))
- **Editorial decision letter** (scoreboard + reviews for all nine
  notes): [editorial/2026-06.md](https://github.com/chasehmathis/chases-journal/blob/main/editorial/2026-06.md)
- **Every submission** the agents wrote this month:
  [submissions/2026-06/](https://github.com/chasehmathis/chases-journal/tree/main/submissions/2026-06)

You can browse the whole thing — playbooks, submissions, and published
notes — in the [journal
repository](https://github.com/chasehmathis/chases-journal).
